commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Verada Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Verada's 6G7x V6 engines are generally robust but suffer from documented weaknesses including head gasket failure, timing belt tensioner issues, and oil pump drive wear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to weigh repair versus replacement costs for your specific Verada variant.

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Mitsubishi Verada 6G72/6G74 Engines Overheat
problems.rootCause: Factory multi-layer steel gasket degrades under thermal cycling; coolant enters combustion chambers causing white smoke and overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace gaskets, machine check, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head undamaged; 30k-50k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72/6G74 with upgraded gaskets, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Verada; 50k+ mile expectancy with warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Verada has under 80k miles and the block is undamaged, a specialist head gasket repair may suffice. For higher mileage or if overheating has warped components, a rebuilt engine with upgraded gaskets offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G74 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Verada

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Risk on Mitsubishi Verada V6 Engines
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner wear or improper installation causes belt jump/snap; all 6G7x engines are interference design.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump; labour-intensive but prevents disaster
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; 60k mile service interval recommended

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after belt failure damage; includes upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if valves/pistons damaged; warranty essential

problems.recommendation: Never gamble with timing belt service on a Verada. If the belt history is unknown, budget for preventative replacement immediately. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine is typically the only viable path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Verada timing belt replacement or engine rebuild

problems.h4: Oil Pump Drive Failure - Silent Killer for Mitsubishi Verada 6G74 Engines
problems.rootCause: Oil pump drive gear wears or shears (documented in ISTA D-21-98), causing sudden oil pressure loss and bearing damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drop sump, replace oil pump and drive, flush system; requires engine removal on some variants
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before bearing damage; monitor oil pressure closely

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G74 with upgraded oil pump drive, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil warning light illuminated; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your Verada's oil pressure light flickers at idle, act immediately. A specialist oil pump replacement may save the engine if addressed before bearing wear occurs. If knocking is present, replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G74 Mitsubishi Verada engine prices with upgraded oil pump

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Failure - Coolant Loss on Mitsubishi Verada 6G74 Engines
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler internal corrosion cracks, allowing exhaust gases into coolant system causing overheating and contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler assembly, flush coolant system, check for head damage
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before overheating damages head gasket

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine if coolant contamination caused bearing or gasket failure
repairOptions.longevity: Required if overheating has warped components or contaminated oil

problems.recommendation: Address EGR cooler leaks promptly on your Verada. If coolant loss is isolated to the EGR system and the engine hasn't overheated, replacement of the cooler alone is cost-effective. If overheating occurred, inspect for secondary damage before deciding.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Verada EGR cooler or engine replacement

problems.h4: Immobiliser/ECU Issues - When Electrical Faults Mimic Engine Failure
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Verada all series (1991-2005, all engine codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; often appears after battery replacement or electrical work
problems.rootCause: Immobiliser control unit or ECU communication failure prevents fuel/spark, causing no-start conditions mistaken for mechanical failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Specialist diagnostics, immobiliser/ECU testing, wiring repair, key reprogramming
repairOptions.longevity: First step for no-start Verada; avoids unnecessary engine replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with matched ECU/immobiliser; complex calibration required
repairOptions.longevity: Only if mechanical failure confirmed alongside electrical issues

problems.recommendation: Before condemning your Verada's engine for a no-start, invest in specialist electrical diagnostics. Many "engine failure" cases are actually immobiliser or ECU communication faults that can be resolved without engine replacement.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Verada engine replacement quotes after electrical diagnosis

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Verada
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Verada's niche status as a grey import means standard UK garages often decline work, but EngineMarket connects you with specialists who understand Australian-spec 6G7x engines. Compare prices from vetted UK Mitsubishi Verada engine suppliers, all offering rebuilt units with upgraded components addressing known failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Nationwide supply & fit available.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Verada and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Verada Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Verada's 1991-2005 production timeline, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The first-generation Verada launched with the 6G72 3.0L V6, establishing the platform but introducing head gasket weaknesses that would affect later engines.
keyChanges: Launch of Australian-built Verada luxury sedan based on Magna platform
TR and TS trim levels with varying equipment levels
knownFor: Adequate performance but heavy fuel consumption
Head gasket failures common after 60k miles
Early models lack later refinements to oil pump drive
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1996 Mitsubishi Verada replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Verada 6G72 engine replacement • KR Series head gasket failure • Verada timing belt cost UK

years.description: The second-generation Verada adopted the larger 6G74 3.5L V6, improving torque but carrying forward head gasket vulnerabilities with new oil pump drive concerns.
keyChanges: Introduction of KH/KW chassis with updated styling and equipment
Replacement of 6G72 with 6G74 3.5L V6 across most variants
Ei and Xi luxury trims added with enhanced features
knownFor: Stronger torque for improved refinement and towing
Head gasket failures persist; oil pump drive wear emerges
EGR cooler cracking documented in later production
Cross-compatibility with UK Diamante parts improves availability
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2000 Mitsubishi Verada replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Verada 6G74 engine replacement • KH Series oil pump failure • Verada EGR cooler cost UK

years.description: The final Verada generation featured updated ECUs, improved emissions equipment, and the rare 6G75 3.8L V6 for performance variants.
keyChanges: Introduction of KE/KF chassis with revised suspension and electronics
6G74 continued as standard; 6G75 3.8L added for GSR performance variant
Updated immobiliser systems requiring specialist calibration post-replacement
knownFor: Refined ride and enhanced luxury features
6G75 offers strong performance but limited UK parts availability
Immobiliser/ECU issues can mimic mechanical failure
Final production years represent best-spec grey import examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2005 Mitsubishi Verada replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Verada 6G75 engine replacement • GSR performance engine cost • Verada immobiliser no start fix

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mitsubishi Verada was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mitsubishi Verada specialists.

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Verada engine in the UK?
items.answer: Specialist UK suppliers handle grey import Verada engines, with EngineMarket connecting you to vetted specialists who understand Australian-spec 6G7x compatibility and cross-reference with UK Diamante parts.

EngineMarket's network specialises in Australian-import engine sourcing

items.question: What engines fit a Mitsubishi Verada?
items.answer: The Verada uses Mitsubishi's 6G7x V6 family: 6G72 3.0L (KR series), 6G74 3.5L (KH/KW/KE/KF), and rare 6G75 3.8L (late KE/KF/GSR), with cross-compatibility to UK-market Diamante engines.
keyPoints: Match engine code to your chassis: TR/TS=6G72, KH/KW/KE/KF=6G74/6G75
UK Diamante engines often interchange with Verada units
Verify ECU/immobiliser compatibility before purchasing replacement
Specialist suppliers can confirm fitment using your registration
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Verada engine compatibility now

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Verada engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Verada engine prices range £1,200-£4,000 supply-only depending on code (6G72 cheapest, 6G75 premium), with supply & fit adding £600-£1,200 for specialist installation.
keyPoints: 6G72 3.0L rebuilt: £1,200-£2,800 typical range
6G74 3.5L rebuilt: £1,500-£3,200 most common price bracket
6G75 3.8L rebuilt: £2,000-£4,000 due to rarity and specialist requirements
Supply & fit service essential for grey import calibration needs
items.cta: Get tailored Mitsubishi Verada engine replacement quotes

items.question: Is a Mitsubishi Verada engine the same as a Diamante?
items.answer: Yes, Verada and UK-market Diamante share identical 6G7x engines, allowing cross-sourcing of replacement units, though ECU/immobiliser calibration may require specialist attention post-installation.
keyPoints: Mechanical components are fully interchangeable between models
UK Diamante engines offer easier sourcing for Verada owners
Immobiliser/ECU pairing may need specialist recalibration after swap
Always verify chassis code compatibility before purchasing donor engine
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Verada and Diamante engine options

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Verada 6G74 head gasket failure?
items.answer: Factory multi-layer steel gaskets degrade under thermal cycling in the 6G74, allowing coolant into combustion chambers; upgraded gaskets in rebuilt engines address this known weakness.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs 70k-110k miles without preventative maintenance
Symptoms include white exhaust smoke, coolant loss, overheating
Rebuilt engines include upgraded MLS gaskets to prevent recurrence
Early detection via coolant monitoring can avoid catastrophic damage
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Verada 6G74 rebuilt engines

items.question: Do you warranty grey import Mitsubishi Verada engines?
items.answer: Yes, every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with terms confirmed directly with your chosen supplier before purchase.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms protect high-use grey import owners
Always confirm exact warranty scope with supplier pre-purchase
Rebuilt units with upgraded components qualify for full coverage
items.cta: Find warranty-backed Mitsubishi Verada engine suppliers

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Verada V6 engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, 6G7x engines can exceed 150k miles, but head gasket and timing belt vulnerabilities require proactive servicing to reach maximum lifespan on grey import Verada.
keyPoints: Timing belt replacement every 60k miles critical for interference engines
Regular coolant and oil changes extend head gasket life significantly
Upgraded components in rebuilt engines improve longevity beyond factory specs
Specialist servicing knowledge essential for Australian-spec ECUs
items.cta: Compare long-life Mitsubishi Verada engine options

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi Verada engine myself?
items.answer: While mechanically possible, Verada engine replacement requires specialist knowledge of Australian-spec immobiliser/ECU calibration, making professional supply & fit the recommended route for grey import owners.
keyPoints: Immobiliser/ECU pairing often fails without specialist diagnostic tools
Engine mount positions vary between KR/KH/KE chassis generations
Specialist garages understand cross-compatibility with Diamante parts
Supply & fit service includes road testing and calibration verification
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi Verada engine supply & fit service

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Verada timing belt failure?
items.answer: Tensioner wear or improper installation causes 6G7x belt jump/snap; all Verada engines are interference design, making preventative replacement every 60k miles essential to avoid catastrophic damage.
keyPoints: Documented in Mitsubishi Service Bulletin TSB-97-12 for early models
Failure results in valve/piston collision requiring full engine rebuild
Rebuilt engines include upgraded tensioners and idler components
Always replace water pump concurrently with timing belt service
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Verada timing belt or engine replacement

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Verada 6G75 better than the 6G74?
items.answer: The 6G75 3.8L offers more torque for performance variants but shares the 6G74's core vulnerabilities; rarity makes parts sourcing and specialist rebuilds more expensive for UK Verada owners.
keyPoints: 6G75 provides 230-245 hp vs 6G74's 200-215 hp for improved performance
Same head gasket and oil pump drive failure patterns apply
Limited UK availability increases cost and lead time for replacements
Best suited for GSR owners committed to long-term specialist maintenance
